tasks.direction_relation_note: Most supersession operator behavior is already shipped, but the pool and distributor still own raw git and worktree command execution inside application services instead of depending on an explicit workspace boundary.
tasks.title: Extract GitWorkspacePort from supersession pool and distributor flows
tasks.description: Move raw git worktree inspection, slot provisioning, branch validation, rebase, and cleanup execution out of `parallel_mode_service` and `parallel_mode_distributor_service` into an explicit outbound boundary so the shipped pool and distributor contracts stop depending on inline process helpers.
tasks.priority_reason: The supersession runtime loop is already shipped, so the clearest remaining gap is replacing raw git orchestration with an explicit workspace seam that matches the architecture direction.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Parallel turn launch and stream handling still route through the shared conversation runtime directly, so supersession has not fully reached the explicit agent-session boundary promised by the architecture direction.
tasks.title: Extract AgentSessionPort from parallel turn launch and stream execution
tasks.description: Introduce an explicit agent-session outbound boundary for new-thread launch, turn streaming, thread-prepared capture, and terminal observation so supersession runtime orchestration stops depending directly on `ConversationService` and TUI stream plumbing.
tasks.priority_reason: After the workspace boundary is explicit, the remaining high-value architecture work is to stop driving supersession through the shared single-session runtime interface.
